headline: Hospital Food Quality Rated Poor by 58% of Inpatients Surveyed
context: MOH patient satisfaction survey found majority of hospital inpatients dissatisfied with food quality and nutrition.

cards.big: 58% of public hospital inpatients rated food quality as poor or very poor in 2025 survey
cards.sub: Hospital food budget is RM8 per patient per day for 3 meals and snacks

cards.big: 23% of hospital inpatients experience malnutrition worsening during their stay
cards.sub: Poor hospital nutrition delays recovery and increases length of stay by average 2.3 days

cards.big: Extra bed days from nutrition-related complications cost public hospitals RM420 million annually
cards.sub: Investing RM4 more per patient daily in food would save RM420 million in extended stays

cards.big: Denmark's New Nordic Hospital Diet improved patient nutrition status 28% and cut waste 40%
cards.sub: Quality hospital food investment generates measurable clinical and financial returns

cards.big: Hospitals that serve food causing malnutrition are undermining the treatment they provide
Should hospital food budgets be doubled and meal quality included in hospital performance metrics
